title: DiscreteRTC: Discrete Diffusion Policies are Natural Asynchronous Executors
summary: DiscreteRTC addresses the asynchronous execution problem in robotic control by proposing discrete diffusion policies as natural replacements for flow-matching-based real-time chunking methods. The approach eliminates the need for specialized fine-tuning, heuristic guidance, and the additional inference costs that prior asynchronous execution methods require. The method achieves improved success rates on dynamic manipulation tasks while reducing computational overhead compared to continuous flow-matching baselines.
